title: Monardella perplexans
intro: Monardella perplexans, also known as Purple Mountain Monardella, is a perennial wildflower native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help soothe digestive issues, reduce pain, and boost the immune system. The bioactive constituents of Monardella perplexans include monoterpenes, sesquiterpenes, and flavonoids, such as rosmarinic acid and carnosic acid, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Monardella perplexans include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support respiratory health, reduce stress, and promote overall well-being.
